text: Code reviews are an important practice in software development. When a developer completes a piece of code, another team member checks it before it is added to the main project. This helps catch bugs early, saving time and effort that would be needed to fix problems later. Beyond bug detection, code reviews promote knowledge sharing within the team. Developers can learn different techniques, new tools, or better ways to solve problems while reviewing others' work. Additionally, code reviews improve overall team quality by encouraging consistency in coding style and best practices. In short, regular code reviews lead to stronger, more reliable software and a more skilled team.
